content.problemStatement:
You are given a permutation (P1,P2,…,PN)(P_1, P_2, \ldots, P_N)(P1​,P2​,…,PN​). In one operation, you can do the following:


Choose any integers L,RL, RL,R with 1≤L≤R≤N1 \le L \le R \le N1≤L≤R≤N. Then, swap the maximum and minimum elements in subarray P[L:R]P[L:R]P[L:R].
Sort the permutation. You can spend at most 4⋅1064\cdot 10^64⋅106 operations in total for all test cases from a single input.


Input Format


The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT −-− the number of test cases. The description of test cases follows.




The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.




The second line of each test case contains NNN integers P1,P2,…,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1​,P2​,…,PN​ −-− the elements of the permutation.





Output Format
For each test case, in the first line output KKK −-− the number of operations.


In each of the next KKK lines, output two integers L,RL, RL,R (1≤L≤R≤N1 \le L \le R \le N1≤L≤R≤N), denoting that you are swapping maximum and minimum in segment P[L:R]P[L:R]P[L:R].


Sum of KKK over all test cases can't exceed 4⋅1064 \cdot 10^64⋅106.
constraints: The sum of N over all test cases ≤ 30 000
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T – the number of test cases. Then for each test case:
- A line with an integer N – the length of the permutation.
- A line with N distinct integers P1,P2,…,PN – the permutation of numbers 1…N.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case output K – the number of performed operations.
Then output K lines, each containing two integers L and R (1 ≤ L ≤ R ≤ N) describing an operation that swaps the maximum and minimum elements of the subarray P[L…R]. The total number of printed operations over all test cases must not exceed 4·10^6.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1 is already sorted, so no operation is needed.
Test case 2: we process values 4,3,2.
- For 4 the segment [1,4] swaps 4 and 1.
- For 3 the segment [1,3] swaps 3 and 2.
- For 2 the segment [1,2] swaps 2 and 1. After three operations the array becomes (1,2,3,4).
Test case 3: value 5 is at position 1, segment [1,5] swaps 5 and 1, sorting the permutation in one operation.

solution.approach:
We place the numbers from the largest to the smallest.
For a value x we already know the position idx where x currently stands (maintained in an auxiliary array pos). If idx equals x, x is already in the correct place. Otherwise we apply one operation on the segment [L,R] where L = min(x,idx) and R = max(x,idx). In this segment all numbers larger than x are already fixed to the right of x, therefore x is the maximum element of the segment and the element at position x is the minimum. Swapping max and min moves x to its correct position x and puts the former element of position x to idx. After the swap we update the permutation and the positions of the two swapped values. Repeating this for x = N … 1 sorts the whole array using at most N operations per test case.
The algorithm runs in O(N) per test case because each value is processed once and all updates are O(1).
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over the whole input (≤ 30 000).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for the permutation and the position array.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        a = [0] * (N + 1)
        pos = [0] * (N + 1)
        for i in range(1, N + 1):
            val = int(next(it))
            a[i] = val
            pos[val] = i
        ops = []
        for x in range(N, 0, -1):
            idx = pos[x]
            if idx == x:
                continue
            l, r = (x, idx) if x < idx else (idx, x)
            ops.append((l, r))
            v1, v2 = a[l], a[r]
            a[l], a[r] = v2, v1
            pos[v1], pos[v2] = r, l
        out_lines.append(str(len(ops)))
        out_lines.extend(f"{l} {r}" for l, r in ops)
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
